nodes.label: Broadcast + Momentum (lean baseline learning rule)
nodes.rationale: The simulator's actually-implemented baseline: direction = forward_sign XOR feedback; weight -= pulse x momentum x direction, where pulse is the whole broadcast loss (scaled only by a per-scope PWM learning-rate knob) and momentum is the sole per-Scap credit signal. A deliberate, recorded simplification that drops the spec's per-level normalized Current-Mirror share routing (§22 #3/#6) - resolving the spec's own §2.4 'routing-update coupling' worry by cutting routing and keeping magnitude.

nodes.label: Cost Inversion (forward expensive / backward free)
nodes.rationale: In ordinary ML the backward pass is expensive; here it is inverted - the ALU sits and waits for capacitors to charge (forward is the costly sequential machine), while backward is one broadcast pulse riding the measurement forward already paid for.
